text:
There was once a very clever goose. She could count backwards from fifty, knit scarves for orphan owls, speak fluent parlez-vous rooster?.

She lived peacefully beside a peaceful lake. One day, an adventurous squirrel fell into the tranquil waters. The goose promptly parachuted down after it. She rescued the thirsty little soul.

The grateful squirrel pledged obedience forever.

There's a whole world out there that feels just... right.

So off they went.

They explored fields of wildflowers, forests filled with singing birds, and enchanted forests.

They met a wise old owl perched atop a oak tree.

"The owl says," owl-eyed owl eyed everything.

"The world is full of wonders."

And that's all they heard.

In time, the squirrel lived happily ever after, forever appreciative.

And the wise old owl?

The very finest mushroom.

You see? Sometimes all you need is... just maybe?
